ab: aimaka kitubulrwa ku musuwa?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: eene, kyahagadha ku palraingi.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: aimaka kitubulrwa ku musuwa?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: ini, bekela ku thingaleli,
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: lu makamulringedele pyacugu su? nau kitububulru amiya ku musuwa, palraingi su?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: eene, nau ikainga tauwana mukalava musuwa.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: ludhaa aipicugu su? apya kitububulruwa ku musuwa ku pathahwakai ki ngudradrekai ka maulai.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於魯凱族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the Rukai.

ab: ludhaa aimulregelrega ku, kadruwa ku ka aiikai tauwana.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: kyalalra ku ku ngyathingale ku kakwakudha ki ngudradrekai amiya musuwa, naikitububulrwa ku musuwa?
ch: A:我聽說您對魯凱族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Rukai, so I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: kadruwa ka thainu ku thingaleli, ai aipelaela ku ku kelretananeli.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: aimaka tukacudua ku? lupiyaku inya mapadudulrua ku tupacase kiruku.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: eene. aikatuma.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: tahapiyanga ku caili su?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: ku cailili tahamabangatenga si valru,
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: yacengecengeana su.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: alabekace! tahudrawdrangengana ku.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: twalai su inu?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: twaladha ku Labuwa.
en: B: I’m from Dawu.

ab: ku tauwane su miyalri ka ngudradrekai?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是魯凱族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also Rukai?

ab: ini, ngubabuculu.
en: B: No, she is a Paiwan.

ab: wathingale su kauhiva ku swaininuana ku vaga?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: wathingala ku kauhiva ku ngubabubuculana si swasungava.
ch: B: 我會說排灣語和布農語。
en: B: I can speak Paiwan and Bunun.

ab: kaiasaa mitiyakihagai ladulruwaku kitububulru musawa, lamatikaiya, luamanema ku twalrisiya lu alupu?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: eene, saicengecenga ku taha alupa ku.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: maelanenga kaitabulruane su kaiasaa matiadalame tuhamuhu.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: kai taiyaneli patengatenga demedeme, pelaela paususulu ki cucungula.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: tapakeakela ikaivai kai takitububulruaneli. maelanenga kai tapelaelane su.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: asikainu, takalaelangenga lubibilrili.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: aidavacengana ku, syadramaku ku akilalra ku makakalra ku maulai lubibilrili.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: eene! lwalranga.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
